tavily.answer: Recent developments in South-West Angola highlight a severe drought exacerbating existing conflict, security, and humanitarian challenges. The United Nations’ food agencies have warned that acute hunger is set to worsen across multiple global hotspots, including South-West Angola, due to conflict, funding shortages, and climate shocks. The situation is compounded by the ongoing drought, which is pushing millions closer to famine. Humanitarian organizations are calling for urgent action to address the deteriorating conditions, which are expected to continue worsening between June and November 2026. The drought has led to significant food insecurity, with many regions facing high levels of acute food insecurity.

items.title: Ramaphosa vows crackdown on xenophobic violence - Reuters
items.snippet: South Africa will crack down on groups behind xenophobic violence, President Cyril Ramaphosa said on Sunday (June 7), as anti-immigrant protests tarnish the country's reputation. * ### 00:31 New Ebola treatment facilities added at DR Congo's Bunia hospital. *

items.title: DRC Ebola hospital set on fire as protesters demand access to bodies of dead relatives - africanews.com
items.snippet: **Violence has erupted in eastern DR Congo's Ituri province after tensions linked to the handling of victims of the latest Ebola outbreak.**. Protesters in the town of Rwampara wanted to recover the bodies of relatives who died of Ebola in order to bury them.
